content:
Case 17:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 44
- Sex: male
- Chief complaint: Scheduled follow-up after alloSCT and CD19 CAR-T for B-ALL, with interest in outpatient treatment options
- Vital signs: Temp 36.9 °C, BP not reported at this visit, HR not reported at this visit, RR not reported at this visit, SpO₂ not reported at this visit.
Setting: Outpatient hematology/oncology follow-up after alloSCT and CAR-T
Capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient in a routine follow-up setting after intensive leukemia therapy. You can review prior history and recent clinical course, perform a targeted examination, and request standard follow-up evaluations appropriate to an ambulatory visit. Decisions focus on surveillance and whether further workup for relapse or complications is indicated.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "prior medical history and details of B-ALL diagnosis and treatment course"}}

response.request: prior medical history and details of B-ALL diagnosis and treatment course

content:
History Physical:
2024-07-04: The patient presented for scheduled follow-up, reporting stable overall condition. He denied fever, night sweats, or weight loss. No exertional dyspnea, abdominal pain, or neurological complaints were reported. The patient informed the team that he had stopped some medications. He actively inquired about “next treatment options,” emphasizing his preference for outpatient-based regimens.
Current medication: aciclovir 400 mg 1-0-1, posaconazole stopped, 1-0-0, cotrimoxazole 960 mg three times per week (taken irregularly), bisoprolol 2.5 mg 1-0-1, ramipril 5 mg 1-0-1, amlodipine 5 mg 1-0-1, atorvastatin 10 mg (stopped), folic acid 5 mg 1-0-0, magnesium 300 mg 1-0-0, and various nutritional supplements.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "results of recent laboratory tests including complete blood count, blood chemistry, and minimal residual disease (MRD) status"}}

response.request: results of recent laboratory tests including complete blood count, blood chemistry, and minimal residual disease (MRD) status

content:
Cbc Chemistry:
Hb 10.6 g/dL, WBC 3.1 × 10⁹/L, Plt 98 × 10⁹/L; MCV 92 fL, MCH 29 pg.
Differential: neutrophils 42%, lymphocytes 48%, monocytes 7%, eosinophils 2%, basophils 1%.
Coagulation: INR 1.1, aPTT 30 sec.
Chemistry: Na 138 mmol/L, K 4.5 mmol/L, creatinine 1.4 mg/dL, BUN 19 mg/dL, AST 28 U/L, ALT 32 U/L, bilirubin 0.9 mg/dL, LDH 220 U/L, uric acid 5.2 mg/dL, CRP 0.6 mg/dL.
Cardiac biomarkers: NT-proBNP 460 pg/mL, troponin T 0.012 ng/mL.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "immunophenotyping results and chimerism analysis from recent bone marrow biopsy"}}

response.request: immunophenotyping results and chimerism analysis from recent bone marrow biopsy

content: {"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Minimal residual disease (MRD)-negative B-ALL in remission after alloSCT and CAR-T therapy", "differentials": ["Chronic GVHD", "Medication-related cytopenias", "Infection-related complications"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Continue immunosuppression taper per institutional protocol", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Monitor CBC, chemistry, and MRD status every 3 months", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Initiate outpatient maintenance therapy discussion including blinatumomab or inotuzumab if MRD becomes detectable", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Minimal residual disease (MRD)-negative B-ALL in remission after alloSCT and CAR-T therapy

treatment_plan.action: Continue immunosuppression taper per institutional protocol
Monitor CBC, chemistry, and MRD status every 3 months
Initiate outpatient maintenance therapy discussion including blinatumomab or inotuzumab if MRD becomes detectable

content: {"evaluation": {"working_diagnosis": "partially_correct", "differentials": ["incorrect", "partially_correct", "partially_correct"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}, {"action": "partially_correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "partially_correct"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}]}}

Round 2 begins.
Setting: Hematology follow-up with bone marrow reassessment
Capabilities: The patient is undergoing more detailed evaluation because of his leukemia history and evolving laboratory findings. You can organize marrow-based assessments and additional consultations to clarify disease status. Some specialized tests are still pending.
Request additional information or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "bone marrow biopsy results including morphology, flow cytometry, and cytogenetic analysis"}}

response.request: bone marrow biopsy results including morphology, flow cytometry, and cytogenetic analysis

content:
Bone Marrow:
Bone marrow biopsy (2024-07-09): The marrow was hypocellular (cellularity 25%) with therapy-related toxic changes. Trilineage hematopoiesis was present but displayed dysplastic features: megakaryopoiesis was reduced with small, hypolobulated forms; erythropoiesis showed megaloblastoid maturation; and granulopoiesis exhibited toxic granulation and nuclear irregularities. There was no blast excess (<5% by morphology).
Flow cytometry revealed a normal distribution of myeloid and lymphoid populations with T-cells showing mild inversion of the CD4/CD8 ratio. A small blast population (0.1%) was detected, characterized by CD19-/CD22-, CD34^dim, CD33-, CD10-, CD20-, CD117+, CD5+, CD7+, CD56-, cyMPO-, cyTdT-, cyCD3-.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "results of chimerism analysis and detailed cytogenetic findings"}}

response.request: results of chimerism analysis and detailed cytogenetic findings

content:
Cytogenetics Chimerism:
Karyotype: 46, XX.
Chimerism: 0.06% recipient.

solve.working_diagnosis: Mixed chimerism with minimal residual disease and therapy-related myelodysplasia following alloSCT and CAR-T

treatment_plan.action: Initiate donor lymphocyte infusion (DLI) for mixed chimerism
Perform comprehensive infectious disease workup for opportunistic infections
Monitor chimerism and MRD every 2-4 weeks

content: {"evaluation": {"working_diagnosis": "partially_correct", "differentials": ["correct", "partially_correct", "incorrect"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}, {"action": "partially_correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "correct"}, {"action": "partially_correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "partially_correct"}]}}

Round 3 begins.
Setting: Relapse and transplant planning conference in a heavily pretreated leukemia patient
Capabilities: You now have results from bone marrow morphology, flow cytometry, clonality assays, and broad molecular profiling together with a comprehensive treatment history including alloSCT, bispecifics, inotuzumab, blinatumomab, and CD19 CAR-T. You are expected to interpret the pattern of MRD relapse, phenotypic evolution, and donor-derived CHIP, and to formulate a realistic outpatient-oriented salvage and transplant-bridging strategy.
Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "B-ALL relapse with mixed chimerism and therapy-related myelodysplasia after alloSCT and CAR-T", "differentials": ["Acute leukemia transformation", "Chronic GVHD with secondary cytopenias", "Infection-related cytopenias"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Initiate blinatumomab for MRD-positive B-ALL", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Continue close chimerism and MRD monitoring every 2-4 weeks", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Evaluate for second alloSCT with alternative donor", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: B-ALL relapse with mixed chimerism and therapy-related myelodysplasia after alloSCT and CAR-T

treatment_plan.action: Continue close chimerism and MRD monitoring every 2-4 weeks
Evaluate for second alloSCT with alternative donor
